propositions: Add a regression test reproducing a detached-HEAD-local-ahead tracker state where HEAD advances past base but refs/heads/tickets lags.
Local-ahead is measured by HEAD rather than the lagging branch ref, and the ff-adopt reset --hard runs only when rev-list {remote}..HEAD is empty.
The pre-WS3 bug measured local-ahead by refs/heads/tickets, read empty, and reset --hard orphaned the un-pushed local commit.
With shared base ancestor and both sides ahead, reconvergence must treat it as a diverged case and perform a union merge.
After sync.reconverge both the local and remote event commits are reachable from HEAD and both event dirs exist in the working tree.
The local commit must not be orphaned and survives an aggressive gc --prune=now.
